title: Tom Ascol Nominated For Southern Baptist Convention President
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States reports the nomination of Tom Ascol, pastor of Grace Baptist Church and president of Founders Ministries, for President of the Southern Baptist Convention, framing the candidacy as a hopeful moment for abolitionists pushing the SBC toward the immediate abolition of abortion. The article gathers endorsements from abolitionist pastors who present Ascol as a sola Scriptura candidate willing to confront the convention's drift on abortion, sexuality, sexual abuse, and critical race theory.
audiences: Southern Baptist messengers weighing how to vote

questions.question: Who nominated Tom Ascol for SBC president and what positions does Ascol currently hold?
questions.audience: Southern Baptist messengers weighing how to vote
questions.answer: Ascol pastors Grace Baptist Church in Cape Coral, FL and leads Founders Ministries. His nomination was put forward by a group of Southern Baptists that included SBC First Vice President Lee Brand, signaling a level of insider support behind the candidacy.
questions.quote: Tom Ascol, Pastor of Grace Baptist Church in Cape Coral, FL and president of Founders Ministries, was nominated for President of the Southern Baptist Convention (SBC) on Tuesday by a group of Southern Baptist pastors and members including SBC First Vice President Lee Brand.

questions.question: What issues has Founders Ministries been pressing inside the SBC, and where does abolition fit in?
questions.answer: Founders has primarily fought feminism and critical race theory inside the convention, and Ascol has more recently turned his voice toward abolition by publicly criticizing SBC leaders who tried to suppress the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion.
questions.quote: Ascol and Founders have become a force within the SBC, primarily fighting against feminism and critical race theory which have been creeping into the convention. In recent months, Ascol has become more outspoken on the subject of abolition, criticizing SBC leaders who tried to squash the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion and who are still trying to oppose it.

questions.question: What is the Ascol family's prior connection to abolitionist work at the SBC Annual Meeting?
questions.answer: Tom Ascol's brother Bill Ascol led the floor effort at the June 2021 SBC Annual Meeting, motioning to bring the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion out of committee and serving as its primary spokesman during debate.
questions.quote: Ascol's brother, Bill Ascol, led the abolitionist charge at the SBC Annual Meeting last June, motioning to bring the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion out of committee and being the primary spokesman on its behalf while it was debated on the floor.

questions.question: How does Dusty Deevers frame the case for Ascol's candidacy?
questions.answer: Deevers, the primary author of the Southern Baptist Resolution on Abolishing Abortion, casts Ascol as the 'sola Scriptura candidate' whose 44-year track record shows he will measure every issue, including abortion, sexuality, and sexual abuse, against the Word of God rather than cultural or elite pressure.
questions.quote: Without reservation, I support Dr. Tom Ascol for President of the Southern  Baptist Convention. Dr. Ascol is the sola Scriptura candidate. Whatever the issue, Tom will do what he's done for 44 years as a SBC pastor: point his finger to the Word of God—not the winds of culture or talking points of the elite—and say, 'By this standard alone!'

questions.question: What complaint about the SBC's trajectory do Ascol's endorsers share?
questions.answer: Endorsers like Brett White argue the convention has drifted from its biblical roots toward pragmatism and needs a leader with a proven record of defending Scripture's authority and sufficiency to steer it back.
questions.quote: Sadly the SBC has strayed from her biblical roots and has long adopted pragmatic methodologies instead of resting on the authority and sufficiency of Scripture. Tom Ascol has a proven track record of being a defender of the truth, and I believe he would have the courage to steer the SBC back to the Bible.

questions.question: What practical change do abolitionist pastors hope an Ascol presidency will produce?
questions.answer: Brett Baggett of Ekklesia Muskogee says messengers need 'men who are not scared to say what God says in His Word and then follow that speaking up with actual doing,' urging fellow SBC messengers to vote for Ascol because he is that kind of man.
questions.quote: I will be voting for Tom Ascol to be the new president of the SBC, and I encourage you to as well, fellow messengers. We need men who are not scared to say what God says in His Word and then follow that speaking up with actual doing. By the grace of God, I think Tom is that kind of man.

questions.question: Beyond abortion, what other doctrinal fights does Ascol's record represent for endorsers?
questions.answer: David Van Bebber points to Ascol's consistent advocacy through Founders Ministries for the SBC to denounce Critical Race Theory and address broader doctrinal drift across the denomination, presenting that record as evidence of the conviction needed for the presidency.
questions.quote: Dr. Tom Ascol has been a consistent voice in advocating a robust response from the SBC to denounce Critical Race Theory and to deal with doctrinal drift  across the denomination. His fortitude, conviction, and love for the SBC have been on display in Founders Ministries for years.

related.title: Prominent Southern Baptist Voice Calls For Total Abolition
related.reason: Earlier Free the States piece highlighting Tom Ascol's public turn toward total abolition, providing the backstory to his 2022 candidacy.

related.title: Southern Baptist Convention Moves One Step Closer to Abolitionism (And Answering Objections)
related.reason: Reports the floor maneuvering led by Bill Ascol that this article references when explaining the family's role in the resolution fight.

related.title: Abolitionists Pass Resolution*, Make Huge Strides at SBC Convention
related.reason: Coverage of the June 2021 SBC vote on the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion that Ascol publicly defended and his nomination builds on.

related.title: The Rise and Progress of Abortion Abolitionism in the Southern Baptist Convention
related.reason: Long-form history of the SBC abolition movement that contextualizes why Ascol's presidency is treated as a movement milestone.

related.title: SBC Pastors Publish "Resolution on Abolishing Abortion" for 2021 Convention Meeting
related.reason: Documents the original publication of the resolution Dusty Deevers authored and that Ascol publicly defended.

related.title: Former ERLC Policy Director Matt Hawkins Glad SBC Squashed Abolition "Like a Bug"
related.reason: Illustrates the SBC establishment opposition to abolition that Ascol's candidacy explicitly challenges.

related.title: Against Pro-Life Compromise: Responding to Denny Burk, Andrew Walker, et al.
related.reason: Engages the SBC pro-life intellectuals whose compromise positions Ascol's nomination is positioned against.
